classes.name: gui4us.view.display.main_panel.Layer1D
documentation:
NOTE: this class is not a part of gui4us API.
This is class is intended only to keep the compatiblitiy
between 1D and 2D displays implementation (see the source code below).

classes.name: gui4us.common.ImageMetadata
documentation:
---
param dtype: 'image data type, acceptable: float32'
param extent: |-
  (extent_oz, extent_ox), in the given units, extent is a pair
  (min, max)
param id: (height label id, width label id)
param shape: (number of pixels OZ, number of pixels OX)
param units: (height units, width units)
---

classes.name: gui4us.cfg.display.Layer2D
documentation:
---
param extent: |-
  image dimensions, a pair (oz_extent, ox_extent),
  each (min, max)
---

classes.name: gui4us.cfg.environment.UltrasoundEnvironment
documentation:
Note: initial TGC curve value is

```
param session_cfg

path to the session configuration file

param tx_rx_sequence

TX/RX sequence. Note: the tgc curve value
(tgc_start, tgc_slope, tgc_curve) will be ignored in favor of
the tgc_curve parameter

param processing

processing implementation

param work_mode

HOST, ASYNC or MANUAL

param capture_buffer_capacity

capacity of the capture buffer

param log_file

path to the output log file, if None, a default path
will be used

param log_file_level

log file severity level

param tgc_curve

initial tgc curve to apply

param tgc_sampling

the distance between TGC curve sampling points
```

methods.name: gui4us.app.Application.close
documentation: Closes all environments and cleanups this instance.

classes.name: gui4us.app.env.EnvironmentApplication
documentation:
An interface to the environment process.

This class is intended to be used only by gui4us internals
(for jupyter notebook interface create a custom View).

```
param cfg_path

path to the environment configuration package; if None,
null (dummy) environment will be created
```

functions.name: gui4us.model.envs.arrus.get_depth_range
documentation:
Returns depth range that covers a given grid of points.

Currently, this function can be considered
as a shortcut for (np.min(grid), np.max(grid)).

classes.name: example.cfg.reconstruction.ReconstructLriWedge
documentation:
NOTE: This implementation works correctly only with SSTA.

Rx beamforming for synthetic aperture imaging.

Expected input data shape: n_emissions, n_rx, n_samples
\:param x_grid: output image grid points (OX coordinates)
\:param z_grid: output image grid points (OZ coordinates)
\:param rx_tang_limits: RX apodization angle limits (given as the tangent of the angle), a pair of values (min, max). If not provided or None, [-0.5, 0.5] range will be used
